headline: Islamic Finance Assets Hit RM1.2T But Financial Inclusion Gaps Persist
context: Malaysia's Islamic finance industry reached RM1.2 trillion in assets in 2025, maintaining its position as the world's largest Islamic capital market, while critics note the system primarily serves wealthy investors rather than achieving its social justice mandate.

cards.big: RM1.2 trillion Islamic finance market — world's largest — but Shariah-compliant micro-credit reaches only 6% of B40 Muslims
cards.sub: Sukuk issuance at RM420 billion in 2025 benefits institutional investors while B40 households can't access basic Islamic lending

cards.big: Islamic banking charges effective profit rates averaging 6.8% — only 0.3% below conventional bank interest rates
cards.sub: Marginal pricing difference undermines the argument that Islamic finance provides a meaningfully distinct economic model

cards.big: Zakat collection across 14 states totalled RM3.8 billion in 2025 but distribution to asnaf reached only 72%
cards.sub: RM1.1 billion in zakat funds held undistributed — administrative inefficiency delays wealth redistribution the system promises

cards.big: Shariah Advisory Council decisions are final and binding with no appeal mechanism for consumers who dispute classifications
cards.sub: 13 state religious authorities maintain separate Shariah compliance interpretations creating regulatory fragmentation

cards.big: Islamic finance that charges the same rates as conventional banking and doesn't reach the poor has form without substance
Should Islamic finance institutions be required to allocate 15% of financing to B40 micro-enterprise lending at below-market rates?
